title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Saudi Arabian crude oil flows
Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports to all destinations are heading for significantly lower volumes in the summer
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p9jlc/341164669?h=SY3B2ImMmGC7IfAMpgojWsi7gntXcRxIBYW7NNR4MKM
In the third week of August, downward pressure continues on the VLCC MEG route to China, while Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports to all destinations are expected to decline significantly over the summer compared to the previous two years. As shown in the chart above, Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports fell in July and August to the lowest level in a similar period in the last two years. The decline follows Saudi Arabia's announcement in early August that it would extend its voluntary cut in oil production by one million barrels per day for another month.

Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports to all destinations are heading for significantly lower volumes in the summer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T234809.808.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t234809808_9e164383c261.png
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p9jlc/341164669?h=SY3B2ImMmGC7IfAMpgojWsi7gntXcRxIBYW7NNR4MKM
In the third week of August, downward pressure continues on the VLCC MEG route to China, while Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports to all destinations are expected to decline significantly over the summer compared to the previous two years. As shown in the chart above, Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports fell in July and August to the lowest level in a similar period in the last two years.

children.title: ‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T235111.527.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t235111527_9db4472d3d9e.png
Downward pressure on VLCC MEG -China rates continued in the third week of August, while Suzemax and Aframax segments experienced a gradual upswing. VLCC MEG-China f reight rates fell to levels around WS45, and remained below the 50 mark for four consecutive weeks.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T235148.900.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t235148900_e2d5c846016a.png
LR2 AG freight rates fell to WS95 this week, down 25 points from early last week, with signs of weaker momentum in the coming days of August.

children.title: Panamax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T235433.632.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t235433632_7ba0a09a280d.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell below WS150, with a steady decline over the last seven weeks.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR1 Steady - MR2 Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T235658.791.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t235658791_2df93df718a4.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent have shown steady momentum since last week at levels around WS190 in mid-week, though it remains to be seen if firmer momentum develops towards the end of the month. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: Dirty (#vessels) - Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-24T235837.412.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-24t235837412_40d6043d03cc.png
Crude oil tanker supply presented a mixed picture in August with some signs of a downward trend, but upward pressure is back in the VLCC segment with numbers nearly exceeding the annual average. VLCC Ras Tanura : The current count is 71 ships, almost on par with the average for the year, with signs of an upward trend in the remaining days of August.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-25T000104.894.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-25t000104894_0f76809a4884.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-25T000237.595.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-25t000237595_844fc647995d.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The current number of ships is currently 6, which is 6 less than the average for the year and 70% less than the peak eight weeks ago. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current ship count is now 27 ships, which is 47% below the last peak in week 29 and below the average for the year since the end of the previous week.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Tonne Days Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-25T000524.297.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-25t000524297_8e533f84dbd7.png
Dirty tonne days: In the second half of August, the percentage increase in demand (tonnage days) in the Suezmax and VLCC segments continuously declined sharply, while an upward trend was gradually observed in the Aframax segment.

children.title: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Decreasing / MR Mixed
section_path: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Decreasing / MR Mixed
children.section_path_text: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Decreasing / MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-25T000655.549.png | assets/2023-08-25_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-25t000655549_06f277f331bc.png
Panamax tonne days: While there was an upward trend in the previous days of August, there was a downward trend in the third week, with the last high recorded seven weeks ago. Clean MR tonne days: In the last days of August, the picture is mixed, with an upward trend in MR1 and a downward trend in MR2.
